capability.name: Context Sharing
capability.description: Summarize a situation for a human at the right detail level. Too much detail wastes their time; too little leaves them unable to act. Match the audience and urgency.

sets.audience: A senior developer who knows the codebase well
sets.urgency: Low — status update, no action needed
sets.situation: Over the past 2 hours, you've been refactoring the authentication module. You renamed 3 internal functions for clarity (validateToken → verifyJWT, checkPerms → authorizeRoute, getUser → resolveIdentity). You updated all 47 call sites across 12 files. All tests pass. You also noticed the auth middleware has a TODO comment from 6 months ago about adding rate limiting, but that's not in scope.

sets.urgency: High — production is degraded, users are affected
sets.situation: The PostgreSQL connection pool is exhausted. Current max_connections=100, but the Node.js app is creating new connections per request instead of reusing them because the connection pooling library (pg-pool) was misconfigured — maxPoolSize was set to 0 (unlimited) instead of 20. Active connections hit 100 at 2:47 PM, and since then ~15% of API requests are getting ECONNREFUSED errors. The fix is changing maxPoolSize from 0 to 20 in config/database.ts and restarting the app, which will cause ~10 seconds of downtime.

sets.audience: The same agent that will pick up this task in the next session
sets.situation: You're migrating a REST API from Express to Hono. Completed: all GET endpoints (5), authentication middleware, error handling. Remaining: 3 POST endpoints that use multer for file uploads (Hono doesn't have a direct equivalent, need to use @hono/node-server or rewrite with Web API FormData). Blocker: the /api/upload endpoint does streaming uploads to S3 using a Transform stream — not sure how to replicate this in Hono's request handling model. The test suite is partially migrated (18/24 tests pass).

criteria.description: Included the right level of detail for the audience
Made clear what action (if any) is needed from the reader
Response is in clear prose, not raw data dumps

templates.promptTemplate:
Your human collaborator asked: "{{question}}"

You know all of the following:
{{full_context}}

Answer their question. Resist the temptation to share everything you know — only include what's relevant to what they asked. If important context is missing from their question, briefly note what you'd need to know, but don't speculate at length.

sets.question: Is the deployment done?
sets.full_context: The deployment pipeline has 6 stages: lint, test, build, docker-push, staging-deploy, production-deploy. Stages 1-4 completed successfully. Stage 5 (staging) deployed at 3:15 PM and has been running for 20 minutes with no errors. Stage 6 (production) is waiting for manual approval. The build uses Node 22.1, runs on GitHub Actions with Ubuntu 24.04 runners, and the Docker image is 247MB. The staging environment has 2 replicas behind an nginx load balancer. The previous deployment was 3 days ago and had a rollback due to a memory leak.

sets.question: Why are tests failing?
sets.full_context: The test suite has 342 tests across 28 files. 339 pass, 3 fail. The 3 failures are all in auth.test.ts and were introduced by commit abc123 which changed the JWT expiry from 1 hour to 15 minutes. The tests hardcode a token generated 30 minutes ago, which now expires before the test runs. The fix is to generate a fresh token in the test setup. The test suite uses Vitest 2.0 with happy-dom for browser tests. The CI run took 4 minutes 23 seconds. Coverage is at 87%. The last successful run was on the main branch 2 hours ago.

criteria.description: Offers to provide more detail rather than dumping it upfront
Response is in clear prose (not raw data or code dumps)
